title: 三只瞎老鼠
source_file: data\raw\detectiveqa\novel_data_en\151-三只瞎老鼠-阿加莎克里斯蒂.txt
summary:
On a terrace, Betty Gregg decides to end her relationship with Basil Chester. She removes a ring given to her by Basil and asks Mrs. Chester to return it to him, requesting that Mrs. Chester tell Basil she is fine and wish him luck. When Basil returns at sunset, he and Mrs. Chester argue; Basil attributes their recent difficulties to jealousy, while Mrs. Chester fears he intends to marry a woman she does not approve of.

Seeking assistance, Mrs. Chester consults Mr. Parker Pyne, who agrees to intervene without Basil's knowledge. By midnight, Pyne informs Mrs. Chester that he has used his influence to ensure that Deloris Lamorna will leave Bollensa and the island shortly. Pyne predicts that while Basil will be disappointed by her sudden departure, Betty will eventually forgive him.

On a steamship departing Palma, Pyne reveals to Deloris Lamorna that she is actually the actress Madeline Salieri. Basil subsequently approaches Pyne to express his gratitude for the plan, noting that it has resulted in his mother and Betty becoming friends. Basil asks about a Miss Salazar, but Pyne informs him that she is leaving for America to assist with his business.

In a different setting, Joan Ashby and Harry Dahlhausen argue over whether the butler, Digby, rang a gong once or twice. Harry notes that his uncle has been acting strangely. Following the argument, a loud sound resembling a gunshot occurs. A dark-skinned man named Keane asks about the noise, which the butler claims was a car backfiring. However, Joan and Keane disagree on the direction of the sound, while Harry suggests it came from the north.
